content_blocks.text: The several constituents of Kalapurusha (कालपुरुष) will be strong or weak in proportion to the strength of the several planets representing them. But Saturn is an exception.

शनौ बलिनि दुःखाभावः अबले दुःखप्राबल्यम्

When Saturn is strong, there is less of misery; when he is weak, misery predominates.
cf. सारावली

verse_data.translation:
When Saturn is strong, there is less of misery; when he is weak, misery predominates.
cf. सारावली

content_blocks.text:
आत्मादयो गगनगैर्बलिभिर्बलवत्तराः ।
दुर्बलैर्दुर्बलास्ते तु विपरीतं शनेः फलम् ॥

verse_data.sanskrit_text:
आत्मादयो गगनगैर्बलिभिर्बलवत्तराः ।
दुर्बलैर्दुर्बलास्ते तु विपरीतं शनेः फलम् ॥
verse_data.topic: Strength of planets and their effects, with Saturn as an exception

content_blocks.text: and the effects of these qualities will reveal themselves during the dasa periods of the planets concerned.
By the word सौख्य used in the text and translated as "happiness' both wealth and 'progeny are also to be understood in addition to health and knowledge. For it is said

धनञ्च सुखपरसाधनत्वात् सुखशब्देन धनमपि गृह्यते । सुखशब्देन पुत्रश्चापत्यमप्युच्यते । पुत्रजन्मविपत्तिभ्यां न परं सुखदुःखयोः ।

There is no greater happiness or misery than the birth or death of one's child.

verse_data.sanskrit_text: धनञ्च सुखपरसाधनत्वात् सुखशब्देन धनमपि गृह्यते । सुखशब्देन पुत्रश्चापत्यमप्युच्यते । पुत्रजन्मविपत्तिभ्यां न परं सुखदुःखयोः ।
verse_data.translation: There is no greater happiness or misery than the birth or death of one's child.
verse_data.topic: Meaning of 'Sukha' including wealth and progeny

content_blocks.text: Jupiter is therefore the giver of health, wealth, progeny and that is why his association and aspect are said to be extremely beneficial.
By the word 'association' used above, occupation of the planet's house is also to be, understood. This will be explained in Adhyaya XIV. infra. Saturn is the giver of all sorts of दुःख (misery).

cf. यवनः
सम्पदो गुरुसंयोगे शनियोगे विपत्तयः ।
चिन्तितव्यं सदा पुंसां सर्वभावानुसारतः ॥

verse_data.sanskrit_text:
cf. यवनः
सम्पदो गुरुसंयोगे शनियोगे विपत्तयः ।
चिन्तितव्यं सदा पुंसां सर्वभावानुसारतः ॥
verse_data.topic: Effects of association with Jupiter and Saturn

content_blocks.text: In a horoscope, the Sun and the Moon must—not to say of the Lagna—be strong; otherwise it is not a satisfactory one. Of these, the Moon is the most important. For, it is said

चन्द्राकौ बलयुक्तौ कुजादयः प्रोक्तकालबलहीनाः ।
शुभफलदातारः सर्वे दशासु योगेषु संचिन्त्याः ॥

verse_data.sanskrit_text:
चन्द्राकौ बलयुक्तौ कुजादयः प्रोक्तकालबलहीनाः ।
शुभफलदातारः सर्वे दशासु योगेषु संचिन्त्याः ॥
verse_data.topic: Consideration of planetary strengths for predicting results

raw_text:
42.
जातकपारिजाते
Adh II
The several constituents of Kalapurusha (कालपुरुष) will be strong or weak in proportion to the strength of the several planets representing them. But Saturn is an exception.
" शनौ बलिनि दुःखाभावः अबले दुःखप्राबल्यम् " When Saturn is strong, there is less of misery ; when he is weak, misery predominates.
cf. सारावली
आत्मादयो गगनगैर्बलिभिर्बलवत्तराः ।
दुर्बलैर्दुर्बलास्ते तु विपरीतं शनेः फलम् ॥
and the effects of these qualities will reveal themselves during the dasa periods of the planets concerned.
By the word सौख्य used in the text and translated as "happiness' both wealth and 'progeny are also to be understood in addition to health and knowledge. For it is said
धनञ्च सुखपरसाधनत्वात् सुखशब्देन धनमपि गृह्यते । सुखशब्देन पुत्रश्चापत्यमप्युच्यते । पुत्रजन्मविपत्तिभ्यां न परं सुखदुःखयोः । There is no greater happiness or misery than the birth or death of one's child.
Jupiter is therefore the giver of health, wealth, progeny and that is why his association and aspect are said to be extremely beneficial.
cf. यवनः
गुरुणा देहपुष्टिः स्याद्बुद्धिपुत्रार्थसम्पदः ।
By the word 'association' used above, occupation of the planet's house is also to be, understood. This will be explained in Adhyaya XIV. infra. Saturn is the giver of all sorts of दुःख (misery). cf. यवनः
सम्पदो गुरुसंयोगे शनियोगे विपत्तयः ।
चिन्तितव्यं सदा पुंसां सर्वभावानुसारतः ॥
In a horoscope, the Sun and the Moon must—not to say of the Lagna—be strong; otherwise it is not a satisfactory one. Of these, the Moon is the most important. For, it is said
चन्द्राकौ बलयुक्तौ कुजादयः प्रोक्तकालबलहीनाः ।
शुभफलदातारः सर्वे दशासु योगेषु संचिन्त्याः ॥
extraction_notes: Minor OCR errors in Devanagari script required correction based on astrological and linguistic context.
